title: Cirrus: Run int. tests in parallel with unit
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

This is somewhat of a band-aid since these two tasks take 45+ minutes
each.  Running them in parallel improves the overall runtime when
tests are otherwise/generally passing.  If/when unit tests fail, the
status won't update until integration tasks complete, but the failure
will be immediately observable in a PR.  So it's a minor price to pay
for improved runtime in the general case.

#### How to verify it

CI will pass

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None - by team request.

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

None

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [cevich] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Note: I've had to re-run the "Fedora int. w/ overlay" tasks twice.

> Note: I've had to re-run the "Fedora int. w/ overlay" tasks twice.

We need to investigate these flakes. They've hit me today as well.
LGTM but flakes still be flakes and it looks like you might need to rebase?
New changes are detected. LGTM label has been removed.
...whoa, well okay, and at the very same second, now it's merged.  OKAY!  Well, let's :pray: together :grinning:
Ref:  CI running on the main branch after merging this: https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/4766513156
Day late dollar short, but here's the new task map: ![cirrus-task-map-buildah](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/148946141-ca613787-7874-4a79-bd85-8262b1265a23.png)
Shouldn't we do cross_build in Smoke test?  And can't we run conformance in parallel with the others.

> Shouldn't we

I thought about this actually, but decided to keep the PR focused only on the problem at hand.  That said, there is some benefit to completely failing (and not continuing to test anything further) for easily detectable/simple problems.  In other words, I don't think we should just parallelize everything.  The reason conformance depends on everything (in my mind) is that it's the most complex (most moving parts).  But I'm willing to be wrong.

So @rhatdan proposal is:

1. Smoke + vendor + cross_build
2. Unit + integration + containerized-integration + Conformance

Theoretical overall runtime savings should be about 15-minutes, unless there's a failure.  In that case, all the non-failing tasks represent wasted compute-time and money (a few nickles).  The other issue is that Cirrus-CI does rate-limit the number of concurrent tasks based on overall system load and if we pay them (we don't).  So in practice it may not actually change much except at night or when few other jobs are executing.

I think perhaps you're missing a few tens of `>` there :wink:   No doubt the parallel-task rate limiting is the real kick in the teeth.  I did look into what it would take to pay Cirrus-CI...but it appears only to work for private repositories :disappointed: Anyway, If that sequence is acceptable, I can certainly give it a try during a busy time, and see what the downside is.
